title: Self-Judgment After Death
description: The universal teaching across traditions that the judgment after death is not external punishment but the soul's own recognition of what it has done. Tibetan: Yama Dharmaraja is a reflection of one's own actions; the mirror of past deeds and white/black pebbles are counted by the innate conscience. Egyptian: the heart weighs itself against Ma'at's feather. Steiner: the pain one caused becomes one's own pain. Tantraloka: karmic coverings are assessed by Śiva as witness. Daimon path: the Guardian shows everything not yet purified.
